name: Monthly Recurring Revenue (MRR)
description: Calculates MRR from active subscriptions, tracking new MRR, expansion, contraction, and churn. The single most important SaaS metric for understanding business health and growth trajectory. Handles subscription upgrades, downgrades, and cancellations.
sql_template:
WITH monthly_subscriptions AS (
  SELECT 
    DATE_TRUNC('month', s.current_period_start) AS month,
    s.id AS subscription_id,
    s.customer_id,
    s.status,
    SUM(
      CASE 
        WHEN p.recurring_interval = 'month' THEN (si.quantity * p.unit_amount) / 100.0
        WHEN p.recurring_interval = 'year' THEN (si.quantity * p.unit_amount) / 100.0 / 12.0
        ELSE 0
      END
    ) AS mrr
  FROM subscriptions s
  JOIN subscription_items si ON si.subscription_id = s.id
  JOIN prices p ON p.id = si.price_id
  WHERE s.current_period_start >= '{{start_date}}'
    AND s.status IN ('active', 'trialing', 'past_due')
  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3, 4
),
monthly_totals AS (
  SELECT 
    month,
    SUM(mrr) AS total_mrr,
    COUNT(DISTINCT customer_id) AS active_customers,
    AVG(mrr) AS arpu
  FROM monthly_subscriptions
  GROUP BY month
)
SELECT 
  month,
  ROUND(total_mrr, 2) AS total_mrr,
  active_customers,
  ROUND(arpu, 2) AS arpu,
  ROUND(total_mrr - LAG(total_mrr) OVER (ORDER BY month), 2) AS mrr_change,
  ROUND(100.0 * (total_mrr - LAG(total_mrr) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / NULLIF(LAG(total_mrr) OVER (ORDER BY month), 0), 2) AS growth_rate_pct
FROM monthly_totals
ORDER BY month DESC
LIMIT {{limit}}

parameters.name: limit
parameters.label: Number of Months
parameters.description: How many recent months to show

author: Sery Team
example_output:
Returns a table with columns:
- `month`: Month (YYYY-MM-01)
- `total_mrr`: Total monthly recurring revenue
- `active_customers`: Count of active paying customers
- `arpu`: Average revenue per user (MRR / customers)
- `mrr_change`: Dollar change from previous month
- `growth_rate_pct`: Growth rate as percentage
